data.title: A trader missed out on $2.6 million in ASTEROID profits.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, 80 days ago, trader 0x5811 spent $542 to buy 7.43 billion ASTEROID tokens. The day before ASTEROID's price surged, this trader sold all 7.43 billion ASTEROID tokens at $405, incurring a loss of $137. Today, the 7.43 billion ASTEROID tokens are worth over $2.6 million.

data.title: Michael Saylor Shares Bitcoin Tracker Update
data.description: On April 19, Michael Saylor, founder of Strategy, released new information regarding the Bitcoin Tracker. According to BlockBeats, Saylor encouraged followers to 'Think Even ₿igger.' Historically, Strategy has disclosed additional Bitcoin holdings the day after such announcements. As of the last disclosure, Strategy holds a total of 780,897 BTC, with an average acquisition cost of approximately $75,577 per Bitcoin.

data.title: SEC Approves FINRA Rule Change, Easing Day Trading Requirements
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has approved a rule change proposed by the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) that significantly alters the requirements for pattern day traders. According to NS3.AI, the new rule eliminates the $25,000 minimum equity requirement for pattern day traders, reverting to the standard $2,000 margin-account baseline.

Additionally, the rule change removes the pattern day trader designation and replaces trade-count limits with intraday margin calculations based on real-time position risk. This adjustment aims to provide more flexibility for traders by allowing margin calculations to reflect current market conditions.

The full implementation of these changes may take up to 18 months, as brokerage firms will need to upgrade their systems to accommodate the new regulations. While the rule change does not directly affect cryptocurrency trading regulations, it could indirectly impact Bitcoin and other digital assets if the eased equity speculation rules increase retail investors' risk appetite.

data.title: Trump says a deal between the US and Iran is still possible: it will happen.
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump told ABC News on the 19th that Iran had "seriously violated" the ceasefire, but he still believed a peace agreement could be reached with Iran. "It will happen. Either way. Either it's negotiable or it's tough. It will happen. You can quote me." (Xinhua)

data.title: A whale deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and increased its BTC short position by 30x.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and increased its short position in BTC (30x). It currently holds 700 BTC, worth $52.89 million, with an entry price of $75,119 and a liquidation price of $80,839.93.

data.title: US President Trump: If Iran does not accept the agreement, the US will no longer be lenient.
data.description: US President Trump: If Iran does not accept (the agreement), the US will destroy every power plant and every bridge in Iran. We will not be polite anymore. (Jinshi)

data.title: Hungary's New Prime Minister Outlines Steps to Restore EU Funding
data.description: Hungary's incoming prime minister has unveiled initial measures aimed at restoring access to European Union funding, which has been frozen due to concerns over corruption and rule of law issues linked to the policies of his predecessor. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of these steps in addressing the EU's concerns and potentially unlocking crucial financial resources for Hungary. The prime minister emphasized the importance of transparency and adherence to EU standards as part of his strategy to rebuild trust and cooperation with the European Union. These efforts are seen as vital for Hungary's economic stability and growth, as the country seeks to align its governance practices with EU expectations.

data.title: Blue Origin Achieves Milestone with Reusable Rocket Booster
data.description: Blue Origin's flagship New Glenn rocket successfully launched into space on its third flight, marking a significant achievement for the Jeff Bezos-backed company. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this mission reused a booster for the first time, a crucial step in Blue Origin's efforts to compete with SpaceX. The successful reuse of the booster represents a milestone in the company's pursuit of cost-effective and sustainable space travel solutions.

data.title: XRP Stays Below $1.40 Despite ETF Inflows
data.description: XRP traded close to $1.35, maintaining its position below the $1.40 resistance level despite experiencing three consecutive days of ETF inflows. According to NS3.AI, CoinGlass data revealed that cumulative ETF inflows reached $1.23 billion. Additionally, futures open interest increased to $2.47 billion on Wednesday, up from $2.38 billion on Monday.

data.title: South Korean Forgery Ring Sells Fake Documents via Telegram
data.description:
A South Korean forgery ring is reportedly selling counterfeit official documents through Telegram, prioritizing payments in cryptocurrency or digital gift cards. According to ChainCatcher, DL News has revealed that the group offers fake Yonsei University diplomas for approximately $200, enrollment certificates for around $100, and counterfeit diplomas from overseas universities for about $341.

The report highlights that the ring also provides forged materials such as driver's licenses, proof of family relationships, certificates of no criminal record, and bank loan documents. South Korean police have noted a significant increase in arrests related to forgery cases, with numbers nearly doubling from 2021 to 2023.

data.title: Trump Accuses Iran of Violating Ceasefire Agreement
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has accused Iran of significantly breaching the ceasefire agreement. According to Odaily, Trump remains optimistic about reaching a peace agreement with Iran.

data.title: Trump claimed that Iran had seriously violated the ceasefire agreement, but still believed that a peace agreement could be reached with Iran.
data.description: According to ABC News: Trump stated that Iran has seriously violated the ceasefire agreement; however, he still believes a peace agreement with Iran is possible. (Jinshi)

data.title: The President of Kyrgyzstan met with Justin Sun; the TRON ecosystem will be fully implemented in Kyrgyzstan.
data.description: On April 18, Kyrgyz President Sadyr Japarov held fruitful talks with Justin Sun, founder of TRON, in the capital Bishkek. The two sides reached broad consensus on the application of TRON infrastructure in Kyrgyzstan, the ecosystem expansion of the national stablecoin KGST, and in-depth cooperation in the fields of AI and blockchain. President Japarov stated that Kyrgyzstan aims to become a regional hub for virtual assets and Web3 technologies in Central Asia. Justin Sun stated that Kyrgyzstan will provide comprehensive strategic support, encompassing specific proposals including joint development of a sovereign large language model, the construction of a cryptocurrency exchange, digital banking services, and the construction of a hydroelectric-driven data center.

data.title: Kyrgyzstan Aims to Become Central Asia's Virtual Asset Hub
data.description:
Kyrgyzstan President Sadyr Japarov held a productive meeting with TRON founder Justin Sun in Bishkek. According to Foresight News, the discussions centered on the implementation of TRON infrastructure in Kyrgyzstan, the expansion of the national stablecoin KGST ecosystem, and deepening cooperation in AI and blockchain sectors.

President Japarov expressed Kyrgyzstan's ambition to become a regional center for virtual assets and Web3 technology in Central Asia. Justin Sun pledged comprehensive strategic support, including proposals for joint development of sovereign large language models, establishment of cryptocurrency exchanges, digital banking services, and hydropower-driven data centers.

data.title: Iranian Forces Block Two Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iranian armed forces have reportedly prevented two oil tankers from passing through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. According to Odaily, the incident was reported by Iran's Tasnim News Agency. The Strait of Hormuz is a strategic chokepoint for global oil shipments, and any disruption in this area can have significant implications for international oil markets.

data.title: Iran's Tasnim News Agency: Iranian armed forces prevented two oil tankers from passing through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday.
data.description: According to Iran's Tasnim News Agency, Iranian armed forces prevented two oil tankers from passing through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. (Jinshi)

data.title: Russia Proposes Severe Penalties for Illegal Crypto Exchanges
data.description: Russia has introduced a draft bill that aims to impose harsh penalties on operators of illegal cryptocurrency exchanges. According to NS3.AI, the proposed legislation could result in up to seven years of forced labor for those found guilty of running unregulated crypto trading platforms. The Russian government has highlighted the significant volume of daily trading in these unregulated markets, estimated at approximately $648 million. The bill is currently awaiting approval and, if passed, is set to become effective on July 1, 2027.

data.title: Lido Earn: Suspension of additional deposits to earnETH due to Kelp DAO vulnerability.
data.description: According to a statement from Lido, the Lido Earn team is aware of the exploitation of the Kelp DAO and that earnETH has exposure to rsETH. As a precaution, they have suspended additional deposits to earnETH and are assessing the situation with relevant partners. More details will be released later.

data.title: Alcoa plans to sell an idle smelter to NYDIG, while Bitcoin mining companies are accelerating their deployment of energy infrastructure.
data.description: Alcoa is reportedly close to a deal to sell its long-dormant Massena East aluminum smelter in New York State to Bitcoin mining company NYDIG. CEO Bill Oplinger stated that the transaction is expected to close by mid-year. The smelter, which closed in 2014 due to rising energy costs and increased global competition, possesses well-developed infrastructure including substations, transmission lines, and high-capacity grid access, making it highly attractive to Bitcoin mining farms and data center operators. Furthermore, the site has access to hydroelectric power from the New York State Electricity Authority, which will help reduce energy costs and enhance its low-carbon profile. This move comes at a time when the trend of transforming industrial land in the United States into digital infrastructure is gaining momentum. Previously, Century Aluminum sold its Hawesville, Kentucky smelter to TeraWulf for $200 million, planning to convert it into a high-performance computing and AI data center. Meanwhile, NYDIG continues to expand its Bitcoin mining footprint and already holds a stake in mining company Coinmint. With mining profits under pressure, several mining companies, including MARA, Hive, and Hut 8, are accelerating their transformation towards AI and cloud computing infrastructure. (Cointelegraph)
